name: Majority-vote LLM Annotation Is Benchmarked Against Expert Human Ratings Before Synthetic Writing Use
statement: Nebhi et al. select LLM annotators by comparing them with expert human grammatical-accuracy ratings, then use a majority-vote ensemble to annotate synthetic writing response pairs for response generation and scoring-model integration.
population_or_context: Adaptive English writing assessment for EFSET.
quantitative_note: The source reports Cohen kappa values of 0.86 for LLaMA 3.3 70B, 0.87 for GPT-4o, 0.78 for Nova Pro, and 0.89 for the selected ensemble against a human-annotated gold standard.
caveats: Human benchmarking is based on approximately 200 item-response pairs with retained inter-annotator agreement above 70%.
The synthetic responses and labels support a particular adaptive writing pipeline, not a universal scoring benchmark.
Majority-vote LLM annotation is not the same as independent human labeling of all synthetic responses.
evaluation_method: Cohen kappa is used to select LLM annotators against expert human ratings before majority-vote annotation of synthetic response pairs.
reported_metrics: Cohen kappa by candidate model and ensemble; synthetic response-pair counts.
limitations: The paper should not be interpreted as validating generated responses without reference to the human gold standard.
The response type is proficiency-aligned English writing, not all student response formats.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The finding paraphrases the expert-human gold standard, LLM annotator Cohen kappa table, majority-vote procedure, and synthetic response-pair generation.
